medical_history.history_of_present_illness: First seizure at 24 years with no evidence of any precipitating factors. Brain MRI shows bilateral periventricular nodular heterotopia. Thrombocytopenia at age 23 years. Mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation at age 32 years.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Ectopic 3rd kidney and patent ductus arteriosus requiring surgical closure at 18 months of age. Thrombocytopenia at age 23 years.
medical_history.family_history: Parents are healthy with normal brain MRI. Younger sister has bilateral periventricular nodular heterotopia but no seizures or cardiac abnormalities.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Brain MRI revealed bilateral multiple and diffuse periventricular nodular heterotopia. Echocardiography revealed a mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Both sisters heterozygous for a c.2002C > T transition in exon 13 of the FLNA gene resulting in a p.Gln668Ter mutation. This mutation was not detected in peripheral blood lymphocytes from the unaffected parents.
Thrombocytopenia noted with complaints of unexplained weight loss. Continued easy bruising and prolonged bleeding time.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Index patient: Bilateral multiple and diffuse periventricular nodular heterotopia (PNH). Sister: Heterotopic gray matter of both hemispheres.
Index patient: Mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation. Sister: Normal echocardiogram.

other_tests.findings: Sister: Did not show any interictal abnormalities.
Verified molecularly using the Identifier kit (Applied Biosystems).

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: X-linked periventricular nodular heterotopia (PNH) due to a loss of function mutation in the filamin A gene (FLNA)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of bilateral periventricular nodular heterotopia observed on brain MRI, a history of seizures, thrombocytopenia, and cardiac abnormalities (mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation). Genetic testing confirmed a heterozygous c.2002C > T transition in exon 13 of the FLNA gene, resulting in a p.Gln668Ter mutation, which is a known disease-causing mutation for PNH. The mutation was not detected in the parents, suggesting germline mosaicism as the likely explanation.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Surgical closure at 18 months of age
Performed at age 23 years after complaints of unexplained weight loss
Revealed bilateral multiple and diffuse periventricular nodular heterotopia
